name: Informes 3r trimestre Lou-Ellie Colas Acceptable Sam.pdf
content:
Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name:                         Lou-Ellie Colas
Teacher:                      Clarisse Bydens
Level:                        Sam 2024/2025
Absences:                     1
Frequency of use (audio):     Pass

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation):     Good
Comment:
Lou-Ellie usually comes to class in a happy, easy-going mood. She takes part in most activities with great enthusiasm. She loves story time and even plays an active role turning the pages of the storybook and sometimes recites the story with the teacher. She also enjoys the "Ants/Bugs counting", the "Have you got Sam's sandwich?", the "Bugs on a picnic" and the "Actions TPR" activities. She is usually quite cooperative with her teacher.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process):     Good
Comment:
Lou-Ellie's English knowledge is good. She knows a decent amount of vocabulary and structures. She can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, she specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. She has also reinforced her knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders. She can also count quite well.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour):     Very good
Comment:
Lou-Ellie is an easy-going, good-humoured, enthusiastic student. She treats the teacher with respect and care. She does sometimes get a bit distracted by external elements and needs to be reminded to stay focused and listen to the teacher. She generally does so without a problem and follows the teacher's instructions very well. She takes good care of the material and gives it back directly when asked.

Marks

Oral Test Mark:     7.67     Acceptable.

---

Final comment
Throughout the school year, Lou-Ellie has mostly been coming to class in a cheerful, happy mood. She has been getting better at staying focused during most of the lesson. She has also made progress in her learning, developing both her memory and her comprehension skills. Therefore, it is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year.

name: Informes 3r trimestre Lina Carboni Very Good Sam.pdf
content:
Kids&Us

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Lina Carboni
Teacher: Clarisse Bydens
Level: Sam 2024/2025
Absences: 1
Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good
Comment:
Lina usually comes to class in a happy mood and a huge smile on her face. She loves story time. She also really enjoys the "Bugs on a picnic", the "Actions TPR" and the "Have you got Sam's sandwich?" activities, as well as any activity involving balloons and face paint. She tends to stay focused and motivated all lesson long, seldom asking when it is time for mum and dad. She is very cooperative with her classmates and her teacher.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Very good
Comment:
Lina's English knowledge is very good. She can also count very well. She knows a decent amount of vocabulary words and structures. She can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, she specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. She has also reinforced her knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good
Comment:
Lina is a joyful, fun-loving and good-humoured student. She treats her classmates and her teacher with care and respect. She generally listens attentively to the teacher and stays focused during the activities, usually following the teacher's instructions very well. She gives the material back when asked, she shares it with her classmates and she takes good care of it without a problem.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 8.80 Very good.

---

Final comment

At the beginning of the school year, Lina was a quiet student who was always in a good mood, but needed to be encouraged to participate in the activities. Now, she has found her place in her group. She is also very motivated to take part in the various activities. Moreover, she has made great progress in her English learning, developing both her memory and comprehension skills. Therefore, it is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year.

name: Informes 3r trimestre Arthur Motte Good Sam.pdf
content:
Kids&Us

Objectives and content of the Sam course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Arthur Motte
Teacher: Clarisse Bydens
Level: Sam 2024/2025
Absences: 5
Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Very good
Comment:
Arthur usually comes to class in a quiet, yet happy mood. He really seemed to enjoy the "Bugs on a picnic" and the "Have you got Sam's sandwich?" activities as well as story time. Arthur's participation has continued to improve throughout the term, as he participated more willingly in the activities. He is very cooperative with his classmates and the teacher. He also stays focused all lesson long.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good
Comment:
Arthur's English knowledge is good. He knows a decent amount of vocabulary and structures. He can sometimes use them in sentences, often requiring the teacher's help to do so. This term, he specifically learnt the different bugs and the different picnic items. He has also reinforced his knowledge of the sizes, the colours, the moods, the types of weather and the genders. Moreover, Arthur can count quite well.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Very good
Comment:
Arthur is a reserved, gentle, pleasant, friendly student. He treats his classmates and his teacher with care and respect. Arthur really seems to feel comfortable in his group. He sometimes tries to copy his classmates' lively behaviour, whilst still listening to the teacher and staying focused on the lesson, when reminded to do so. He always treats the material with care, shares it with his classmates and gives it back to the teacher directly when asked.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.80 Good.

---

Final comment

Arthur took a while before he felt comfortable in his new group. He slowly came out of his shell and has now developed friendly bonds with his classmates. Moreover, Arthur's English learning and his oral test results have kept improving too. Well done, Arthur! It is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt during the school year.
